title: Coelogyne dempoensis
taxonomy: Coelogyne dempoensis is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. This genus, Coelogyne, consists of around 170 species of terrestrial orchids. They are native to tropical and subtropical regions, particularly in Asia, with a preference for shaded and humid environments. The classification of Coelogyne dempoensis further categorizes it under the subfamily Epidendroideae. Further classification puts it under the tribe Coelogyneae.
uses: Coelogyne dempoensis is known to have medicinal properties in traditional Asian medicine. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, headache, and skin conditions. It is also believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and swelling. Additionally, Coelogyne dempoensis is thought to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, contributing to its potential medicinal applications. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and benefits.

intro: Coelogyne dempoensis, also known as Dempo's Coelogyne, is a species of orchid native to the Himalayas. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine and is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne dempoensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often made into decoctions, infusions, or powders, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and cold, as well as to promote overall well-being and longevity.
